channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Lawmaker Highlights Pakistan's Potential as Mediator in Iran Tensions
item.description: In a virtual briefing organised by the Congressional Pakistan Caucus on Thursday evening, Congressman Tom Suozzi, the Democratic co-chair of the caucus, stated that Pakistan is emerging as a potential...

item.title: Institutional Failures Behind Harmful ADF Interrogation Training
item.description: A lawyer representing over 200 current and former Australian Defence Force members has revealed decades-long exposure to harsh and abusive Resistance to Interrogation and Conduct After Capture trainin...

item.title: Land disputes dey shake food supply for Ibadan and Abuja, causing higher prices and insecurity
item.description: A new report wey SBM Intelligence release don show say violent land disputes dey seriously affect Nigeria food distribution system, especially for Ibadan and Abuja. The study, titled *The Price of a P...

item.title: Dr Nchimbi Represents Tanzania at OACPS Summit to Outline National Priorities
item.description: Vice-President Dr Emmanuel Nchimbi has arrived in Malabo, Equatorial Guinea, where he will represent President Samia Suluhu Hassan at the 11th Summit of Heads of State and Government of the Organisati...

item.title: Repatriation of undocumented Afghans halted due to security incident at Torkham border
item.description: On Thursday night, a serious security incident occurred at the Torkham border where a Frontier Corps (FC) official got injured in a cross-border attack from the Afghan side. Following this, authoritie...

item.title: Broos calls on Bafana Bafana to rediscover their competitive edge ahead of World Cup
item.description: Bafana Bafana coach Hugo Broos has called on his squad to show the same determination and commitment they displayed during their successful Afcon qualification and World Cup campaigns, as they prepare...

item.title: Saraki Rules Out 2027 Presidential Bid To Respect Zoning Arrangement
item.description: Former Senate President Bukola Saraki don talk say e no go run for 2027 presidential election. Saraki yan this one for Channels Television’s Politics Today on Thursday. According to am, e decision dey...

item.title: Government's Mobile Fuel Quota Plan Aims to Protect Poor as Oil Prices Soar
item.description: The editorial discusses the government’s new proposal to launch a mobile app-based fuel quota system aimed at providing targeted subsidies for low-income motorbike and rickshaw users. This digital sys...

item.title: Discovery of a New Arctic Rhino Species Sheds Light on Migration and Evolution
item.description: Researchers from the Canadian Museum of Nature have identified a previously unknown species of rhinoceros in the Canadian High Arctic, named Epiatheracerium itjilik. The fossil, dating back 23 million...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange and IFC Launch Programme to Boost Women in Leadership
item.description: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) imeungana na International Finance Corporation (IFC) kuendeleza utawala wa kike unaojumuisha na uwazi katika biashara. Ushirikiano huu unaonyesha umuhimu wa kuanzish...

item.title: Study finds reducing unnecessary medicines in frail elderly can be safe and beneficial
item.description: A recent global review, published in the journal 'BMC Geriatrics', suggests that cutting down on certain long-term medicines in very frail elderly patients may be both safe and beneficial. Many older ...

item.title: Female students robbed on college bus outside Karachi medical college
item.description: Three armed men riding a single motorbike allegedly robbed female students aboard a college bus parked outside Jinnah Medical and Dental College in Karachi. The suspects held up the students and snatc...

item.title: Government sets May 2027 deadline for Arusha roads ahead of AFCON 2027
item.description: The Tanzanian government has issued a strict directive that roads leading to the Samia Suluhu Stadium in Arusha must be completed by May 2027 with no extensions allowed. This comes as the country prep...

item.title: Paralysed assault survivor Noelia Castillo Ramos chooses euthanasia after years of suffering
item.description: Noelia Castillo Ramos, a 25-year-old Spanish woman, passed away by euthanasia at a hospital near Barcelona after enduring years of physical and psychological suffering following a gang rape. Paralysed...

item.title: Actress Morin Closes Her Clothing Shop After a Year, Citing Personal Lessons and Renewal
item.description: Kenyan TikToker and budding actress Morin has closed her clothing shop just a year after launching it in 2025. The business, which had opened with much excitement and support from her friends Carol So...

item.title: The Rise of AI and Drone Warfare in Modern Conflicts
item.description: It has been a month since the US, along with its Zionist ally, started bombing Iran, resulting in thousands of civilian casualties and severe damage to one of the world's oldest civilisations. Despite...

item.title: North Korea and Belarus Formalise Friendship and Cooperation Treaty
item.description: North Korea and Belarus have signed a Treaty of Friendship and Cooperation during Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko’s first visit to Pyongyang. Both countries, already under Western sanctions ...

item.title: Resumption of Afghan Repatriation at Torkham Border After Month-Long Closure
item.description: The process of repatriating Afghan nationals through the Torkham border resumed on Thursday following a month-long closure due to recent clashes between Pakistan and Afghanistan. Officials at a tempor...

item.title: Pamela Anderson and Aerie Team Up to Promote Authentic, AI-Free Fashion Campaign
item.description: Pamela Anderson has collaborated with fashion brand Aerie to advocate for authenticity and resist the rise of artificial intelligence in advertising. According to People magazine, the partnership is p...

item.title: Heavy rains in Rungwe cause landslides killing 20 people
item.description: Mbeya region, especially Rungwe District, has suffered tragic landslides caused by heavy rains and strong winds early Wednesday. The death toll has now reached 20 people, including two children from K...
